zones.label: IR Distance Sensor
facts: Output: binary digital — LOW when an object is detected, HIGH when clear
Pins: VCC (3.3–5 V), OUT, GND
Adjustable detection range via on-board potentiometer
Typical range: 2 to 30 cm (varies with object and surface)
Fast response; small and low cost
Works best on reflective surfaces
Best for: obstacle avoidance and line-following robots

zones.label: HC-SR04 Ultrasonic
facts: Sends a 40 kHz ultrasonic pulse and times the echo's round trip
Pins: VCC (5 V), TRIG, ECHO, GND
Range: 2 cm to ~400 cm; accuracy ±3 mm
Trigger: ≥10 µs HIGH pulse; echo ≈ 58 µs per cm
Beam angle: < 15°
Inexpensive and easy to use
Best for: robot navigation, level sensing, parking sensors

zones.label: VL53L0X Laser ToF
facts: Laser time-of-flight; I2C interface (up to 400 kHz)
Pins: VCC (2.6–5.5 V), GND, SCL, SDA, XSHUT, GPIO1
Range: 3 to 120 cm default (up to 200 cm in long-range mode)
Accuracy ±3% (best case); field of view ~25°
Fast reading up to 50 Hz; excellent ambient-light immunity
Works on most surfaces, including fabric and glass
Best for: precision distance measurement

quiz.question: Which distance sensor uses a laser and time-of-flight for the highest precision?
quiz.explanation: The VL53L0X times how long a laser pulse takes to bounce back (time-of-flight). It is accurate to about ±3% and works even on fabric or glass.

quiz.question: Which sensor only reports 'object detected or not' rather than an exact distance?
quiz.explanation: The IR reflective sensor gives a binary digital output — LOW when something is in range, HIGH when clear. It does not report an exact distance.

quiz.question: Which sensor bounces a 40 kHz sound pulse and times the echo to find distance?
quiz.explanation: The HC-SR04 sends an ultrasonic burst at 40 kHz and measures the echo's round-trip time. Distance in cm is about the echo time in µs divided by 58.

quiz.question: Which sensor has the longest range (up to about 400 cm) for general robot navigation?
quiz.explanation: The HC-SR04 measures from 2 cm out to about 400 cm, the longest range of the three, which makes it popular for obstacle avoidance.

quiz.question: Which sensor talks over I2C and stays accurate even on fabric or glass?
quiz.explanation: The VL53L0X uses I2C and laser time-of-flight, so unlike ultrasonic it is not fooled by soft or angled surfaces such as fabric and glass.
